Disable Nagle's algorithm\n48\t        os.environ.setdefault('SO_KEEPALIVE', '1')  # Enable TCP keepalive\n49\t\n50\t        logger.info(\"TCP optimizations configured: TCP_NODELAY enabled, keepalive enabled\")\n51\t    except Exception as e:\n52\t        logger.warning(f\"Failed to configure TCP optimizations: {e}\")\n53\t\n54\t\n55\t# Initialize TCP optimizations at module load time\n56\ttry:\n57\t    configure_tcp_optimizations()\n58\texcept Exception as e:\n59\t    logger.debug(f\"TCP optimization deferred to runtime: {e}\")\n60\t\n61\t# ZAI proxy endpoint — overridable via env var for local dev\n62\tZAI_PROXY_URL = os.environ.get(\n63\t    \"ZAI_PROXY_URL\",\n64\t    \"https://zai-proxy-mcp-apexalgo-iad-ts.ardenone.com:8444/v1/messages\",\n65\t)\n66\t\n67\t# Default model for bead formulation\n68\tDEFAULT_MODEL = \"claude-sonnet-4-20250514\"\n69\t\n70\t\n71\t# --- DNS Caching ---------------------------------------------------------------\n72\t\n73\t@lru_cache(maxsize=32)\n74\tdef _resolve_hostname_cached(hostname: str, port: int, timeout: float = 2.0) -> Optional[str]:\n75\t    \"\"\"\n76\t    Resolve hostname to IP with caching.\n77\t\n78\t    Reduces DNS lookup overhead for repeated connections to the same endpoint.\n79\t    Uses LRU cache with TTL expiration implicitly via cache size limits.\n80\t\n81\t    Args:\n82\t        hostname: The hostname to resolve\n83\t        port: The port number\n84\t        timeout: DNS resolution timeout in seconds\n85\t\n86\t    Returns:\n87\t        IP address string or None if resolution fails\n88\t    \"\"\"\n89\t    try:\n90\t        # Use getaddrinfo with socket-level timeout\n91\t        results = socket.getaddrinfo(hostname, port, proto=socket.IPPROTO_TCP)\n92\t        if results:\n93\t            ip_address = results[0][4][0]  # Extract IP from (host, port) tuple\n94\t            logger.debug(f\"DNS resolved: {hostname} -> {ip_address}\")\n95\t            return ip_address\n96\t    except socket.gaierror as e:\n97\t        logger.warning(f\"DNS resolution failed for {hostname}: {e}\")\n98\t        return None\n99\t    except Exception as e:\n100\t        logger.error(f\"DNS resolution error for {hostname}: {e}\")\n101\t        return None\n102\t\n103\t\n104\tdef extract_host_from_url(url: str) -> Optional[tuple[str, int]]:\n105\t    \"\"\"\n106\t    Extract hostname and port from URL.\n107\t\n108\t    Args:\n109\t        url: The URL to parse\n110\t\n111\t    Returns:\n112\t        Tuple of (hostname, port) or None if parsing fails\n113\t    \"\"\"\n114\t    try:\n115\t        parsed = urlparse(url)\n116\t        hostname = parsed.hostname\n117\t        port = parsed.port or (443 if parsed.scheme == \"https\" else 80)\n118\t        if hostname:\n119\t            return hostname, port\n120\t    except Exception as e:\n121\t        logger.warning(f\"Failed to parse URL {url}: {e}\")\n122\t        return None\n123\t\n124\t\n125\tclass ModelClass(Enum):\n126\t    \"\"\"Available model classes.\n127\t\n128\t    Performance note: For routing tasks, SONNET is empirically faster than HAIKU\n129\t    (median ~2362ms vs ~3861ms in our tests). Use SONNET for router despite HAIKU\n130\t    being marketed as \"fast\". SONNET provides better price-performance for this use case.\n131\t    \"\"\"\n132\t    HAIKU = \"claude-haiku-4-20250514\"  # Fast, cheap - but slower for routing in practice\n133\t    SONNET = \"claude-sonnet-4-20250514\"  # Balanced - fastest for routing tasks\n134\t    OPUS = \"claude-opus-4-20250514\"  # Highest quality - for complex tasks\n135\t\n136\t\n137\t@dataclass\n138\tclass LLMRequest:\n139\t    \"\"\"A request to the LLM.\"\"\"\n140\t    system_prompt: str\n141\t    user_message: str\n142\t    model: str = DEFAULT_MODEL\n143\t    max_tokens: int = 4096\n144\t    temperature: float = 0.7\n145\t\n146\t    def to_payload(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n147\t        \"\"\"Convert to API payload.\"\"\"\n148\t        return {\n149\t            \"model\": self.model,\n150\t            \"max_tokens\": self.max_tokens,\n151\t            \"temperature\": self.temperature,\n152\t            \"system\": self.system_prompt,\n153\t            \"messages\": the LLM.\"\"\"\n162\t    content: str\n163\t    model: str\n164\t    input_tokens: int\n165\t    output_tokens: int\n166\t    finish_reason: str | None = None\n167\t    timing_network_ms: float | None = None  # Network latency (first byte received)\n168\t    timing_total_ms: float | None = None  # Total round-trip time\n169\t\n170\t    @property\n171\t    def total_tokens(self) -> int:\n172\t        return self.input_tokens + self.output_tokens\n173\t\n174\t    @property\n175\t    def timing_inference_ms(self) -> float | None:\n176\t        \"\"\"Estimated model inference time (total - network).\"\"\"\n177\t        if self.timing_total_ms is not None and self.timing_network_ms is not None:\n178\t            return max(0, self.timing_total_ms - self.timing_network_ms)\n179\t        return None\n180\t\n181\t\n182\tclass LLMError(Exception):\n183\t    \"\"\"Base exception for LLM errors.\"\"\"\n184\t    pass\n185\t\n186\t\n187\tclass LLMTimeoutError(LLMError):\n188\t    \"\"\"LLM \"\"\"\n199\t    Client for ZAI proxy (llm-proxy.ardenone.com).\n200\t\n201\t    Provides single-turn, stateless LLM calls for:\n202\t    - Escalate strand: bead body formulation\n203\t    - Future: timeout\n215\t        self._client: Optional[httpx.AsyncClient] = None\n216\t\n217\t    async def _get_client(self) -> httpx.AsyncClient:\n218\t        \"\"\"Get or create HTTP client with HTTP/2 and optimized connection pooling.\"\"\"\n219\t        if self._client is None:\n220\t            # Configure aggressive connection pooling for reduced latency\n221\t            # - HTTP/2 for multiplexing and connection reuse\n222\t            # - Larger connection pool for concurrent requests\n223\t            # - Longer keepalive to reduce TLS handshakes\n224\t            # - Connection warmup for faster first requests\n225\t            limits = httpx.Limits(\n226\t                max_keepalive_connections=50,  # Increased from 30 for better concurrency under load\n227\t                max_connections=150,  # Increased from 100 to handle parallel requests\n228\t                keepalive_expiry=180.0  # Increased from 120s to reduce reconnections (3 minutes)\n229\t            )\n230\t\n231\t            # Configure timeout with aggressive connection settings\n232\t            timeout_config = httpx.Timeout(\n233\t                connect=8.0,  # Reduced from 10s for faster failure detection\n234\t                read=30.0,  # Read timeout\n235\t                write=8.0,  # Reduced from 10s for faster failure detection\n236\t                pool=3.0,  # Reduced from 5s for faster failover\n237\t            )\n238\t\n239\t            # Pre-resolve hostname using DNS cache for faster initial connections\n240\t            host_info = extract_host_from_url(self.proxy_url)\n241\t            if host_info:\n242\t                hostname, port = host_info\n243\t                resolved_ip = _resolve_hostname_cached(hostname, port)\n244\t                if resolved_ip:\n245\t                    logger.debug(f\"Using cached DNS resolution: {hostname} -> {resolved_ip}\")\n246\t\n247\t            # Try HTTP/2 with fallback to HTTP/1.1\n248\t            try:\n249\t                # HTTP/2 configuration optimized for low latency\n250\t                self._client = httpx.AsyncClient(\n251\t                    timeout=timeout_config,\n252\t                    verify=False,\n253\t                    limits=limits,\n254\t                    http2=True,  # Enable HTTP/2 for multiplexing\n255\t                    headers={\n256\t                        \"Connection\": \"keep-alive\",\n257\t                        \"Accept-Encoding\": \"gzip, deflate\",  # Enable compression\n258\t                        \"Accept\": \"*/*\",\n259\t                        # Add HTTP/2 optimization headers\n260\t                        \"te\": \"trailers\",  # Enable trailer headers support\n261\t                    },\n262\t                    # Enable socket options for better performance\n263\t                    # httpx doesn't expose socket options directly, but we can set them via event loop policies\n264\t                )\n265\t                logger.info(\"ZAI client initialized with HTTP/2, compression, and optimized connection pooling\")\n266\t            except Exception as e:\n267\t                logger.warning(f\"HTTP/2 not available, falling back to HTTP/1.1: {e}\")\n268\t                self._client = httpx.AsyncClient(\n269\t                    timeout=timeout_config,\n270\t                    verify=False,\n271\t                    limits=limits,\n272\t                    http1=True,\n273\t                    headers={\n274\t                        \"Connection\": \"keep-alive\",\n275\t                        \"Accept-Encoding\": \"gzip, deflate\",  # Enable compression\n276\t                        \"Accept\": \"*/*\"\n277\t                    }\n278\t                )\n279\t                logger.info(\"ZAI client initialized with HTTP/1.1, compression, and optimized connection pooling\")\n280\t        return self._client\n281\t\n282\t    async def  async def warmup(self) -> None:\n289\t        \"\"\"\n290\t        Warm up the connection pool proactively.\n291\t\n292\t        Establishes a low-cost connection to reduce latency for first real request.\n293\t        This eliminates the TLS handshake cost from the first actual LLM call.\n294\t        \"\"\"\n295\t        if self._client is None:\n296\t            client = await self._get_client()\n297\t\n298\t        try:\n299\t            # Send a lightweight OPTIONS request to establish connection\n300\t            import time\n301\t            warmup_start = time.monotonic()\n302\t            response = await self._client.request(\n303\t                \"OPTIONS\",\n304\t                self.proxy_url,\n305\t                headers={\"Content-Type\": \"application/json\"}\n306\t            )\n307\t            warmup_ms = (time.monotonic() - warmup_start) * 1000\n308\t\n309\t            # Check HTTP version from response\n310\t            http_version = getattr(response, 'http_version', 'unknown')\n311\t            pool = self._client._transport._pool\n312\t            protocol = \"HTTP/2\" if getattr(pool, '_http2', False) else \"HTTP/1.1\"\n313\t\n314\t            # Log warmup success (we expect 401/403 or similar, we just want the connection)\n315\t            logger.info(\n316\t                f\"ZAI client connection warmup completed in {warmup_ms:.0f}ms \"\n317\t                f\"(status: {response.status_code}, protocol: {protocol}, response_version: {http_version})\"\n318\t            )\n319\t        except Exception as e:\n320\t            logger.warning(f\"ZAI client warmup failed (non-fatal): {e}\")\n321\t\n322\t    async def call(self, request: LLMRequest) -> LLMResponse:\n323\t        \"\"\"\n324\t        Make a single-turn LLM call.\n325\t\n326\t        Args:\n327\t            request: The LLM request\n328\t\n329\t        Returns:\n330\t            LLMResponse with content, usage info, and timing breakdown\n331\t\n332\t        Raises:\n333\t            LLMTimeoutError: If request times out\n334\t            LLMRateLimitError: If rate limited\n335\t            LLMError: For other errors\n336\t        \"\"\"\n337\t        import time\n338\t        client = await self._get_client()\n339\t\n340\t        request_start_ms = time.perf_counter() * 1000\n341\t        first_byte_ms = None\n342\t\n343\t        try:\n344\t            payload = request.to_payload()\n345\t            logger.debug(f\"LLM request: model={request.model}, input_tokens_estimate={len(request.user_message) // 4}\")\n346\t\n347\t            # Use streaming to measure actual first-byte time (network latency)\n348\t            async with client.stream(\n349\t                \"POST\",\n350\t                self.proxy_url,\n351\t                json=payload,\n352\t                headers={\n353\t                    \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n354\t                },\n355\t            ) as response:\n356\t                # Check for rate limit\n357\t                if response.status_code == 429:\n358\t                    raise LLMRateLimitError(\"Rate limited by ZAI proxy\")\n359\t\n360\t                response.raise_for_status()\n361\t\n362\t                # Measure first-byte time: time when response headers are received\n363\t                first_byte_ms = time.perf_counter() * 1000\n364\t                network_ms = first_byte_ms - request_start_ms\n365\t\n366\t                # Read the full response body (required for streaming responses)\n367\t                response_text = await response.aread()\n368\t                import json as json_lib\n369\t                data = json_lib.loads(response_text)\n370\t\n371\t            request_end_ms = time.perf_counter() * 1000\n372\t\n373\t            # Log HTTP/2 usage for this request\n374\t            http_version = getattr(response, 'http_version', 'unknown')\n375\t            pool = client._transport._pool\n376\t            protocol = \"HTTP/2\" if getattr(pool, '_http2', False) else \"HTTP/1.1\"\n377\t            logger.debug(f\"LLM request using {protocol} (response HTTP version: {http_version})\")\n378\t\n379\t            # ZAI proxy wraps the Anthropic response under \"result\"\n380\t            payload_inner = data.get(\"result\", data)\n381\t\n382\t            # Extract content from response\n383\t            content = payload_inner.get(\"content\", [])\n384\t            if content and isinstance(content, list) and len(content) > 0:\n385\t                text = content[0].get(\"text\", \"\")\n386\t            else:\n387\t                text = str(content)\n388\t\n389\t            # Extract usage\n390\t            usage = payload_inner.get(\"usage\", data.get(\"usage\", {}))\n391\t            input_tokens = usage.get(\"input_tokens\", 0)\n392\t            output_tokens = usage.get(\"output_tokens\", 0)\n393\t\n394\t            # Calculate timing breakdown with actual network measurement\n395\t            total_ms = request_end_ms - request_start_ms\n396\t            inference_ms = total_ms - network_ms\n397\t\n398\t            logger.debug(\n399\t                f\"LLM response: input_tokens={input_tokens}, output_tokens={output_tokens}, \"\n400\t                f\"timing_total_ms={total_ms:.2f}, timing_network_ms={network_ms:.2f} (measured), \"\n401\t                f\"timing_inference_ms={inference_ms:.2f} (measured)\"\n402\t            )\n403\t\n404\t            return LLMResponse(\n405\t                content=text,\n406\t                model=payload_inner.get(\"model\", request.model),\n407\t                input_tokens=input_tokens,\n408\t                output_tokens=output_tokens,\n409\t                finish_reason=payload_inner.get(\"stop_reason\"),\n410\t                timing_total_ms=total_ms,\n411\t                timing_network_ms=network_ms,\n412\t            )\n413\t\n414\t        except asyncio.TimeoutError as e:\n415\t            raise LLMTimeoutError(f\"LLM request timed out after {self.timeout}s\") from e\n416\t        except httpx.TimeoutException as e:\n417\t            raise LLMTimeoutError(f\"LLM request timed out: {e}\") from e\n418\t        except httpx.HTTPStatusError as e:\n419\t       except Exception as e:\n426\t            raise LLMError(f\"LLM request failed: {e}\") from e\n427\t\n428\t    async def call_simple(\n429\t        self,\n430\t        system_prompt: str,\n431\t user_message: User message\n443\t            model: Model to use (defaults to client's default_model)\n444\t            max_tokens: Maximum tokens to generate\n445\t            temperature: Sampling             \"output_tokens\": response.output_tokens,\n468\t            }\n469\t        return response.content\n470\t\n471\t    async def call_streaming(\n472\t        self,\n473\t        system_prompt: str,\n474\t        user_message: str,\n475\t        model: str | None = None,\n476\t        max_tokens: int = 4096,\n477\t        temperature: float = 0.7,\n478\t    ) -> AsyncGenerator[Union[str, Dict[str, Any]], None]:\n479\t        \"\"\"\n480\t        Stream LLM response for progressive card fill.\n481\t\n482\t        Yields text chunks as they arrive from the temperature=temperature,\n503\t        )\n504\t\n505\t        client = await self._get_client()\n506\t\n507\t        try:\n508\t            payload = request.to_payload()\n509\t            payload[\"stream\"] = True  # Enable streaming\n510\t\n511\t            logger.debug(f\"LLM streaming request: model={request.model}\")\n512\t\n513\t            async with client.stream(\n514\t                \"POST\",\n515\t                self.proxy_url,\n516\t                json=payload,\n517\t                headers={\"Content-Type\": \"application/json\"},\n518\t            ) as proxy\")\n522\t\n523\t                response.raise_for_status()\n524\t\n525\t                accumulated_text = []\n526\t                input_tokens = 0\n527\t                output_tokens = 0\n528\t\n529\t                # Process server-sent events\n530\t                async for line in response.aiter_lines():\n531\t                    if not line.strip():\n532\t                        continue\n533\t\n534\t                    if line.startswith(\"data: \"):\n535\t                        data_str = line[6:]  # Remove \"data: \" prefix\n536\t\n537\t                        if data_str                   text = delta.get(\"text\", \"\")\n551\t                                if text:\n552\t                                    accumulated_text.append(text)\n553\t                                    yield text\n554\t\n555\t                            # Extract final usage\n556\t                            if data.get(\"type\") == \"message_stop\":\n557\t 0)\n560\t\n561\t                        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n562\t                            logger.warning(f\"Failed to parse streaming event: {e}\")\n563\t\n564\t                # output_tokens={output_tokens}\")\n567\t                yield {\n568\t                    \"text\": full_text,\n569\t                    \"input_tokens\": input_tokens,\n570\t                    \"output_tokens\": output_tokens,\n571\t                    \"finish_reason\": \"stop\",\n572\t                }\n573\t\n574\t        except asyncio.TimeoutError as e:\n575\t            raise Get the internal httpx client\n        http_client = await client._get_client()\n\n        # Verify HTTP/2 is enabled in the pool configuration\n        pool = http_client._transport._pool\n        assert hasattr(pool, \"_http2\"), \"Pool should have HTTP/2 attribute\"\n        assert pool._http2 is True, \"HTTP/2 should be enabled in pool\"\n\n        await client.close()\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_http2_fallback_on_error(self):\n        \"\"\"Verify fallback to HTTP/1.1 if HTTP/2 initialization fails.\"\"\"\n        # Mock httpx.AsyncClient to raise an exception on first call (HTTP/2)\n        # but succeed on second call (HTTP/1.1 fallback)\n        original_init = httpx.AsyncClient.__init__\n\n        call_count = {\"count\": 0}\n\n        def mock_init(self, *args, **kwargs):\n            call_count[\"count\"] += 1\n            if call_count[\"count\"] == 1 and kwargs.get(\"http2\"):\n                # Simulate HTTP/2 initialization failure\n                raise Exception(\"HTTP/2 not available\")\n            # Let HTTP/1.1 succeed\n            return original_init(self, *args, **kwargs)\n\n        with patch.object(httpx.AsyncClient, \"__init__\", mock_init):\n            client = ZAIClient()\n            # Clear any existing client to force re-initialization\n            client._client = None\n            http_client = await client._get_client()\n\n        # Should have fallen back to HTTP/1.1\n        pool = http_client._transport._pool\n        assert pool._http2 is False, \"Should fall back to HTTP/1.1\"\n        await client.close()\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_http2_headers_present(self):\n        \"\"\"Verify HTTP/2 optimization headers are present.\"\"\"\n        client = ZAIClient()\n        http_client = await client._get_client()\n\n        # Check for HTTP/2 optimization headers\n        headers = http_client.headers\n        assert \"Connection\" in headers or \"te\" in headers, \"Should have HTTP/2 optimization headers\"\n\n        await client.close()\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_connection_pooling_with_http2(self):\n        \"\"\"Verify connection pooling is configured for HTTP/2.\"\"\"\n        client = ZAIClient()\n        http_client = await client._get_client()\n\n        # Check connection limits - stored in the pool attributes\n        pool = http_client._transport._pool\n        assert pool._max_keepalive_connections >= 50, \"Should have large keepalive pool for HTTP/2\"\n        assert pool._max_connections >= 150, \"Should have large max connections for HTTP/2\"\n\n        await client.close()\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_global_client_has_http2(self):\n        \"\"\"Verify the global ZAI client also has HTTP/2 enabled.\"\"\"\n        client = get_zai_client()\n        http_client = await client._get_client()\n\n        pool = http_client._transport._pool\n        assert pool._http2 is True, \"Global client should have HTTP/2 enabled\"\n        await client.close()\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_http2_with_actual_proxy_check(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Verify HTTP/2 negotiation with the actual ZAI proxy.\n\n        This test makes a real connection to verify HTTP/2 negotiation works.\n        It's marked as integration test and may be skipped in CI.\n        \"\"\"\n        client = ZAIClient()\n\n        try:\n            # Warmup to establish connection\n            await client.warmup()\n\n            # Get the client after warmup\n            http_client = await client._get_client()\n\n            # If we got here without exception, HTTP/2 or HTTP/1.1 is working\n            # The logger will have indicated which protocol was used\n            assert http_client is not None, \"Client should be initialized\"\n\n            await client.close()\n        except Exception as e:\n            pytest.skip(f\"Unable to connect to ZAI proxy: {e}\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    pytest.main([__file__, \"-v\", \"-s\"])\n","numLines":134,"startLine":1,"totalLines":134}}}
